_meta.name: Phoenix Omega — Qwen-Image-Layered text-to-layers decompose (manga V5)
_meta.description: V5 architecture (replaces V4 L0+L2 split). Renders ONE full manga panel from a single archetype-shaped text prompt and decomposes it into N clean RGBA layers (default: 2 = background + character) via Qwen-Image-Layered (Alibaba native, late-2025). Output: a batch of N images, written to the staging dir as <prefix>_layer_NN.png. Downstream V5 orchestrator (scripts/manga/render_v5_episode.py) reassembles per archetype subject_placement_bbox. Eliminates the L0+L2 coordinate-system mismatch documented in artifacts/research/manga_layer_compositing_research_2026-05-20.md.
_meta.vram_uncertainty_note: fp8mixed claims fit in 24GB per Alibaba; 16GB on RTX 5070 Ti is empirical-feasibility-gated (see V5 PR cover and research §9.2). If OOM, fall back to CPU-offload via UNETLoader weight_dtype='fp8_e4m3fn'.
_meta.layer_semantics: Qwen-Image-Layered default emits 2 layers: layer_0=foreground subject (RGBA cut), layer_1=background scene (RGB). 3-layer mode (layer_0=character / layer_1=mid-objects / layer_2=background) requires layers=3 on EmptyQwenImageLayeredLatentImage.

inputs.text: {{negative_prompt}}, text, words, letters, captions, watermark, signature, signs, writing, typography, text characters, font, lettering, logos, subtitles, calligraphy, chinese characters, japanese characters, kanji, hiragana, katakana, hangul
